mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in INDPRO signals improving real output and operating leverage for listed firms; higher industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest value 104.3882 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO growth is followed by nonpositive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, labor-market slack signals weaker household income and earnings risk; this threshold predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest value 6.2 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates that household spending is supported by job creation; this demand-labor confirmation predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest value 439802.0 as of t-1mo
fact:PAYEMS latest value 139004.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-growth state has lower average 60-day returns than states without joint confirmation over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% combined with rising INDPRO indicates policy support during real activity recovery; low discount rates and improving output predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 0.09 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not outperform the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return by at least 1 percentage point on average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% signals rising inflation pressure before policy normalization; higher expected discount rates predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest value 237.693 as of t-2mo
fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 0.09 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if CPI acceleration has no negative marginal effect after conditioning on FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: HOUST crossing below 900 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and cyclical demand; this deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest value 893.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-900 triggers are followed by above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if the effect reverses in two rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth paired with a falling UNRATE signals tightening labor conditions and near-term income support; this labor confirmation predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0 and UNRATE month-over-month change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest value 139004.0 as of t-1mo
fact:UNRATE latest value 6.2 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-improvement trigger produces a 5-day hit rate at or below 50% over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth with negative RSAFS growth signals that headline output is lagging a current consumption slowdown; this divergence predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest value 15985.7 as of t-4mo
fact:RSAFS latest value 439802.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if this divergence state has nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns and no lower downside hit rate than the unconditional sample in walk-forward testing.
